observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Budget Office and DILG to coordinate in adhering to the posting requirements and subsequently send a written notice when said reports had been posted on its portal to the House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance, and other offices.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget, in coordination with DILG, to adhere posting and notification requirements to the appropriate offices.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented

No posting of the said report on the FDDP was found.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to:

Direct the immediate posting of all contracts awarded for Emergency Procurement under Republic Act (RA) No. 11469 (Bayanihan to heal as One Act) in the GPPB online portal.

Direct the Municipal General Services Office and BAC to be aware of any circulars/memorandum/advisories of the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) with the reporting or posting requirements in relation to government procurement.

Henceforth, strict compliance to any circulars/memorandum/advisories of the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB).
observations.managementAction: The Management to post all contracts awarded for Emergency Procurement in the GPPB Online Portal and comply with all guidelines of the GPPB, thereafter.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented

No posting of transactions on the GPPB online portal was found.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management to immediately create an Internal Audit Service to assist the management in ensuring that resources are used consistent with laws, regulations and policies; resources are safeguarded against loss or wastage and misuse; financial and non-financial information are accurate and timely; and operations are economical, efficient and effective.
observations.managementAction: Management to create an Internal Audit Service
observations.reason:
Not implemented

Plantilla Positions were created but no personnel were hired.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Adjust the Cash in bank accounts effecting the following proposed journal entries:
Communicate with the AGDB those items which require confirmation and adjust the books accordingly.
Conduct close supervision of the personnel in-charge in the preparation of BRS.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Inventory Committee in coordination with the Municipal Assessor to conduct validation of the existence of said land additions.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of accounts the adjustments for the proper valuation of land items that the Inventory Committee validates at their appropriate fair market value should the acquisition cost be unavailable.
Henceforth, strict adherence to the proper valuation of items of Property, Plant and Equipment provided in Paragraphs 26 and 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standard (IPSAS)17 is enjoined.

We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to book the expenses on the year they were incurred and consistently apply matching principle and accrual basis of accounting.
Henceforth, strict compliance with IPSAS 1 and Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree (P.D) 1445.

We recommended that Management:
Require Local Health Board and the Chief of Hospital to submit the list of public health workers in relation to the grant of COVID-19 SRA; and
4.8.2   Require the Human Resource Office to submit an alternative work arrangement plan duly approved by the head of the agency or equivalent document to show proof that the personnel is authorized to report to the workstation during the state of national emergency, which places them in direct contact with COVID-19 patients.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Local Health Board and the Chief of Hospital of the Carrascal Primary Health Care Facility to submit a certified list of personnel (medical/allied-medical/other personnel) who are catering directly to COVID-19 patients from September 2020 to June 2021 and the record of patients attended.
Require the Chief of Hospital of Carrascal Primary Health Care Facility to submit an explanation detailing the events/circumstances wherein these 39 administrative and other personnel are in direct contact with COVID-19 patients in those number of days they claimed in the payroll from December 2020 to June 2021.
We recommended that Management will cause a refund to the identified public health workers the amount deducted from their COVID-19 SRA.

We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Prepare an action plan to properly addressed the issues and concerns that hindered the implementation of the mentioned projects in order to fast track the implementation thereof; and
Henceforth, require the Municipal Planning and Development Office to advise the Local Development Council to include in their considerations the abovementioned issues during the planning of the budget to prevent delays in the implementation and completion of projects, thereby timely attaining socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Direct the MDRRMO to justify the low utilization rate and the huge accumulated balance of LDRRMF.
Direct the MDRRMO to expedite the implementation of the PPAs.
Henceforth, strict compliance to the provisions of RA10121 in the usage of LDRRMF.

We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountants Office (MAO) and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (MDRRMO) to submit the required quarterly reports on receipt and utilization of LDRRMF, cash donations, and in-kind donations for CY 2021 in prescribed format to the NDRRMC through the OCD, copy furnished the COA Auditors.
Direct the MAO and MDRRMO to coordinate and relay relevant information as necessary and be informed of the required quarterly reportorial requirements as provided for in COA Circular 2014-002 and COA Circular 2020-009.
Henceforth, strict compliance to any circulars/memorandum/advisories related to the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF and donations.

We recommended the following and the Management agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the contractors requesting advance payment/mobilization fee to post/submit a standby letter of credit or a guarantee/security instrument prior to processing the payment thereof; and
Henceforth, always ensure to observe strict compliance for all matters relevant in the implementation of infrastructure projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to submit the lacking documents enumerated herein to support the validity and propriety of the transactions.
Henceforth, strict compliance to the provisions set forth under Section 9.1.1.4 and 9.1.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommend that management cause the immediate submission of the contracts and their integral parts to facilitate auditorial review and evaluation of the contract, and any deficiencies can be rectified on time.

We recommended management to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of accounts the identified understatement/overstatement by drawing the proposed adjusting journal entry.

Direct the Municipal Engineer the immediate submission of the appropriate estimated useful life of assets based on their operation. 

Henceforth, observe inter-office coordination and communication for a cohesive and organized record-keeping of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of accounts the proposed adjusting entries and the Municipal Engineer to establish the appropriate estimated useful life of assets.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that:

Municipal Accountant ensures that all disbursement vouchers relating to land purchase are adequately supported with necessary documents as prescribed in Section 13.1 COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, before processing the payments; and

Municipal Assessor secures all the necessary documents in the transfer of the legal property rights of real property to the Municipal Government.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor to secure the proper requirements for land acquisition and the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of documentary requirements, especially on similar transactions.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Transfer of Land Titles is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:

Be more cautious in booking entries to minimize errors and prevent misinformation to third-party users.
Adopt Perpetual Inventory System in accounting for inventories.

Henceforth, strict observance of the GAM for LGU and other regulations in the proper keeping of accounts is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: The Management to strictly observe the GAM for LGU and other regulations in the proper keeping of accounts.
observations.reason:
Fully Implemented

Accounts are adjusted and taken up in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to require the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the accounting and reporting requirements of COA Circular No. 2014-002, particularly in recognition of donated funds and maintenance of the required Subsidiary Ledger.
observations.reason:
Fully Implemented

The account is taken up in the Reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Secretary of the Municipal Mayor to:

Immediately prepare the required summary/list of donations received, distributed and balances for donations in-kind with its required attachments to the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD), copy furnished to the respective COA Auditors.

Exert effort to update themselves with the current issuances affecting their work, particularly the guidelines on donations in cash and in-kind as provided by the relevant and competent government agencies.
observations.reason:
Fully Implemented

Reiterated/ Discussed in AAR 2021

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Immediately prepare the required one-time report for cash donations and cause the timely submission to National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC), through the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD), copy furnished the respective COA Auditors.

Exert effort to update themselves with the current issuances affecting their work, particularly the guidelines on donations in cash and in-kind as provided by the relevant and competent government agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant to prepare one-time report for cash donations and to cause the timely submission of the same to the NDRRMC through the OCD.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

No one-time report for Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council was furnished to the Auditor’s Office, yet.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Designate appropriate personnel to prepare Business Plan for the existing LEEs to be presented to LCE for approval.

Direct the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an ordinance creating LEEs based on the BP approved by the LCE.

Henceforth, strict compliance to the provisions of Local Budget Circular No. 111.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Preparation of the Business Plan is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan to ensure that local tax rates are updated every five years pursuant to Section 191 of the Local Government Code.

Direct the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan to enact the revision of the Local Revenue Code for immediate imposition.
Henceforth, strict compliance to the Local Government Code.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

On-going revision of the Revenue Code.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Direct the OIC-Municipal Economic Enterprise Development and Management Office (MEEDMO) and the Municipal Treasurer to lobby/coordinate with the Office of the Sanggunian Bayan for the enactment of an ordinance authorizing the imposition of fees and charges for the added services of the Carrascal Adventure Park and Public Market Extension and revision of the local ordinance for the operation of Carrascal Waterworks System and Carrascal Public Market to include the changes to its fees and charges.

Henceforth, strict compliance to the Local Government Code and other issuances relating to the imposition of fees and charges is enjoined.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Created a Management Committee for the establishment of LEE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Direct the Market Supervisor and MEEDMO to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 24 of the Local Ordinance No. 3, s. 2004 and employ the "one stall per person" policy.

Direct the Market Supervisor and MEEDMO to employ other revenue-boosting measures like the prompt posting of vacancies to conspicuous places without prejudice to other local ordinance provisions.

Henceforth, strict compliance to any local ordinances.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Preparation of the Business Plan is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Direct the Market Supervisor and MEEDMO to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 12 of the Local Ordinance No. 3, s. 2004 and start enforcing the parking fee.

Direct the Market Supervisor and MEEDMO to coordinate at all times, especially when problems arise in enforcing policies. 

Henceforth, strict compliance to any local ordinances.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Preparation of the Business Plan is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Direct the Market Supervisor, MEEDMO, and other Market Committees to work hand in hand and enforce the delinquent rental payments' payments.

Direct the Market Supervisor to impose the cancellation/revocation of contracts strictly should the lessee becomes delinquent for two (2) months.

Direct the Market Supervisor, MEEDMO, and other Market Committees to also consider lessees with businesses other than resto-bars in the baywalk stalls since the latter's income is only seasonal.

Henceforth, strict compliance with Ordinance no. 3, s. 2004 and other related local ordinances.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Preparation of Business Plan is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Direct the MEEDMO, Market Supervisor, and Market Committees to reassess the currently implemented rates and formally submit a proposal to amend thereof to the Local Sanggunian.  

Henceforth, strict compliance to ordinances is hereby enjoined.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Preparation of Business Plan is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:

Remind the MBO to exercise prudence and coordinate with the Municipal Development Council in the preparation of Supplemental Budgets and assure its consistency with the approved AIP.

Henceforth, strict compliance with R.A. No. 7160 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: The Management to observe strict compliance with R.A. No. 7160.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:

a. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and send demand letters to the recipient NGO/PO through the implementing office for the immediate submission of liquidation reports;

b. Require the Implementing Offices to have proper monitoring of the implementation of the transfers to NGO/PO; and

Henceforth, strict compliance of Section 5.4 of the COA Circular 2007-001 is hereby enjoined.
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

Only 8 out of 13 NGO/POs submitted liquidations totaling P1,430,000.00

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:

- Direct the Planning and Engineering Department to coordinate with each other in institutionalizing a system of monitoring of the implementation of various PPAs of the Municipal Government, both infrastructure and non-infrastructure, through the creation of dedicated composite inspectorate team with personnel from both offices; and

- Direct the preparation and submission of a monthly monitoring report on publicized government projects/programs/activities providing all the vital information under COA Circular Number 2013-004.
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

No monitoring reports were prepared for non-infra PPAs.

observations.recommendation:

direct the Municipal Accountant to institute a mechanism such as assigning personnel which reports directly to him so that submission of accounts can be properly monitored; and

Henceforth, follow the regulations in the submission of accounts strictly.

We recommended that Management: 

Request, in writing, all depository banks to furnish the bank statements for all depository accounts maintained by the Municipal Government within five days after the end of each month.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the said bank statements with the General Ledgers as bases for the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements which must be submitted regularly to this Office within ten days after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Management strictly adheres to the full implementation of RA No. 9003 and hasten the establishment of the sanitary landfill to ensure the protection of the Municipal’s constituents from the possible ecological impact.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to impose the required minimum net take-home pay for FY 2019 of P5,000.00. By doing so, this will give awareness to employees about their personal finances and help them manage it.

We recommended to the various implementing offices of the Municipality to properly plan, closely monitor, and conduct proper supervision by adopting effective strategies, procedures and controls to ensure timely accomplishments of planned projects, programs and activities. Likewise, strict adherence to the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 relative to the appropriation and utilization of 20% Development Fund to ensure efficient output that would improve more the socio-economic condition of the locality is enjoined.

We recommended to the Management to adopt adequate control methods, procedures and measures in the handling of repair and maintenance of transportation and equipment to safeguard resources of government against possible loss due to waste, abuse, mismanagement, errors, fraud and irregularities.

We recommended to the Municipal Government to adhere to the requirements of RA 9344 and prepare Programs, Projects and Activities of the Local Council for the Protection of Children and to allocate one percent (1%) of the IRA to strengthen its implementation.

We recommended that management require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit to the GPPB the procurement monitoring report within the time frame stipulated in Section 12.2 of the IRR of RA 9184 so that monitoring and evaluation of the Municipal Government’s compliance to the provisions of the Procurement Act can be made.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to finish the conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) to be reconciled with the Accounting records.   Thereafter, submit the Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE shall create an inventory committee headed by the Municipal Treasurer who shall conduct the physical inventory taking of all Municipal properties. Thereafter the committee shall prepare a Report of Physical count of PPE’s to be submitted to the office of the auditor. Further, the report should be reconciled with the accounting record to determine correctness of the balances appearing in the Financial Statement. Any discrepancy should be investigated and be charged against accountable person for any losses, wastage or misuse.

We recommended the Management to immediately conduct of Annual Physical Inventory of all PPE owned by the Municipality. It should be reconciled with the records of Accounting Section. Any discrepancy should be investigated to determine liability to which the equipment was issued.

We recommended to the Management to adopt adequate control methods, procedures and measures in the handling of repair and maintenance of transportation and equipment to safeguard resources of government against possible loss due to waste, abuse, mismanagement, errors, fraud and irregularities.

We recommended that the Budget Section of the Municipal Government strictly adhere to the requirements of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 that all development projects to be funded under the 20% DF shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio economic development and environmental management outcome of the LGU.

We recommended to the Municipal Government to adhere to the requirements of RA 9344 and prepare Programs, Projects and Activities of the Local Council for the Protection of Children and to allocate one percent (1%) of the IRA to strengthen its implementation.

We recommended MDRRMO to prepare a comprehensive MDRRM Plan for its program, projects and activities to ensure and strengthen the disaster preparedness, prevention, mitigation and response of the province. Also, establish a structured monitoring system to track down the implementation of various PPAs for DRRM to check whether these were achieved according to its expected results.

We recommended to the Management to immediately create an Internal Audit Service to assist the management in ensuring that resources are used consistent with laws, regulations and policies; resources are safeguarded against loss or wastage and misuse; financial and non-financial information are accurate and timely; and operations are economical, efficient and effective.

We recommended to the various implementing arms of the Municipal Government to properly plan, closely monitor and conduct proper supervision by adopting effective strategies, procedures and controls to ensure timely accomplishment of planned projects, programs and activities.

Recommendation:
Direct the Accountant to update the preparation and/or submission of the monthly bank reconciliation statements of all depository accounts maintained by the province within the prescribed deadlines pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011: and implement measures that will enable the Municipality to meet the deadlines. Likewise, prepare the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to record the reconciling items affecting the accounts.
observations.managementAction: All FS with attach Bank Recon. Statements were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Municipal Engineering Office to review the reasonableness ofApproved Budget for the Contract (ABC)on the on-going procurements and adhere to the provisions of DPWH Department Order No. 22 Series of 2015 for the upcoming projects.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to finish the conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) to be reconciled with the Accounting records.   Thereafter, submit the Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of Section 7.2.1a of the COA Circular No. 2009-006 by submitting disbursement vouchers and financial reports within the timeframe.

We recommended to the Management to strictly adhere with the requirement of Local Government Code of the Philippines and record the collections as part of the General Fund where proper appropriation ordinance should be issued before it can be disbursed.

We recommended that the management should request the Sangguniang Bayan to authorize the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer the total amount of ₱1,220,055.24 to either Land Bank of the Philippines or Development Bank of the Philippines to protect government funds.

We recommended that the LCE shall create an inventory committee headed by the Municipal Treasurer who shall conduct the physical inventory taking of all Municipal properties. Thereafter the committee shall prepare a Report of Physical count of PPE’s to be submitted to the office of the auditor. Further, the report should be reconciled with the accounting record to determine correctness of the balances appearing in the Financial Statement. Any discrepancy should be investigated and be charged against accountable person for any losses, wastage or misuse.

We recommended that the management should strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 that contracts and POs should be submitted to COA five days after the execution of contracts or POs. Further, inspection reports for delivered items should be provided to the auditors within 24 hours after inspection and delivery to ensure adherence to internal control system and procedures in safeguarding government resources.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office should compel the contractor to submit the necessary records, reports and document immediately to facilitate validation, verification and inspection to avoid the amount suspended totaling ₱1,833,297.59 to mature into disallowance.

We recommended that BAC should stop the practice of procuring goods and services through “shopping” and should strictly adhere to the requirements that Bidding should be the primary mode of procurement using the available APP as guide and basis

We recommended to the various implementing divisions of the Municipality to properly plan, closely monitor, and conduct proper supervision by adopting effective strategies, procedures and controls to ensure timely accomplishments of planned projects, programs and activities.

We recommended to the Management to fast track the needs to make the equipments operational. Further, management should ensure meticulous planning of urgent and very important needs of the Municipality to maximize utilization of government resources.

Municipal Treasurer’s Office should facilitate the titling of the parcels of land owned by the Municipal Government. Also, the audit team required the MTO and Accounting Office to complete and reconcile their records/reports.

We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to observe Section 59 (c) of Local Treasury Operations Manual particularly such control measure of:
1)  Stamping serial numbers or the name or logo of the municipality to the cash tickets before issuing to assigned collector;
2) 	Practice of affixing initials of collector and date of issuance in the cash tickets before issuance; and
3) 	Tearing off of cash tickets after collections has been received to avoid possible re-issuance.

We recommended that management require the Local School Board to strictly follow the provisions of Section 101 & 272 of Republic Act (RA) 7160 in the preparation of the budget and utilization of Special Education Fund (SEF).
We recommended that management adhere strictly with the provisions of Section 27 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act (RA) 9184.
We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of Section 37.1.3 in all procurements made by the Municipality.

We recommended the Management to direct the Inspectorate Team and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that delivery of goods should be accompanied by warranty security and in the absence thereof, at least 10% of the amount paid should be withheld as retention money to cover for defects on delivered items.

We recommended that Mangement direct the Municipal Budget Officer to establish separate Registry of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for the Quick Response Fund (QRF), Maintenance and Other Operating Exppense (MOOE) and Capital Outlay (CO) of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF).

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format under COA Circular 2012-002 on a monthly basis and require the LDRRMO to submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council to the COA Auditor.

We recommended to the management to formulate Agency’s Annual Gender and Development Accomplishments in accordance with the procedures and prescribed format under Paragraph 5.2 of the DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

We recommended that management meticulously and judiciously plan the budgeting and implementation of the projects funded under the 20% Economic Development Fund in order to optimally utilize the fund and eventually help the Municipality in attaining its desired goals and objectives.
